title: Boston → The West → Boston
subtitle: A Tesla Model Y National-Parks Field Guide

stages.name: Boston to the Black Hills
stages.notes: The short Day 2 arrival (~5–5.5 hr) gives you extra time in Cuyahoga Valley this year — fold the afternoon into Day 3 if you like. Charging here is effortless — just top off in any I-90 town. Sage Creek Campground is free, first-come, and bison wander through; worth the camp night.

days_list.route: Boston → Watkins Glen, NY (~5.5–6 hr; I-90 W to Albany, Thruway, then south to Seneca Lake)
days_list.charge: Geneva, NY or Horseheads/Big Flats (no Supercharger in Watkins Glen)
days_list.sleep: Watkins Glen village or Corning, NY hotel

days_list.route: Watkins Glen → Cleveland, OH (~5–5.5 hr; back to I-90 via Geneva/Waterloo, west through Buffalo & Erie)
days_list.sleep: Cleveland hotel — suburban chain (Independence/Beachwood) or near I-90/I-271, not downtown budget

days_list.route: Cuyahoga Valley NP — free park; Brandywine Falls, Ledges Trail
Cleveland → Chicago area — transit day
days_list.sleep: Chicago-area hotel — suburban (Schaumburg/Naperville/near I-90), not city budget

days_list.route: Chicago → Madison, WI (~2.5 hr, ~150 mi) — arrive ~lunchtime; afternoon + evening in town
days_list.sleep: Madison hotel — stay on/near the isthmus (Capitol Square / State Street) for easy walking; same-day booking reliable. (Splurge: The Edgewater on Lake Mendota)

days_list.route: Madison → Sioux Falls, SD (~6 hr, ~415 mi; the fuller leg but under the cap)
days_list.sleep: Sioux Falls hotel — optional evening: Falls Park (Big Sioux River over pink quartzite, lit at night) + downtown Phillips Ave (SculptureWalk, Arc of Dreams, dinner); optional morning: Falls Park in daylight or Palisades State Park (~25 min NE, scenic quartzite)

days_list.route: Sioux Falls → Badlands NP — arrive afternoon, sunset over spires
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Badlands/White River KOA (Interior — full hookups, showers; 50-amp site charges overnight)

days_list.route: Badlands hiking — Notch, Castle, Door & Window trails
Black Hills — Mt. Rushmore, Wind Cave NP (free), Black Elk Peak
Buffer / explore — Custer State Park wildlife loop, Needles Hwy
Black Hills → Cody, WY (~5 hr; Sheridan, WY is the alternative overnight if preferred) — Beartooth / Chief Joseph byway optional
Cody → West Yellowstone (~3 hr via East Entrance over Sylvan Pass, ~80 mi) — charge FULL in Cody before the climb (treat usable range as ~60–70% of rated on Sylvan Pass)
days_list.sleep: West Yellowstone hotel (8-stall Supercharger in town — top off overnight)

days_list.route: Yellowstone (3 days) — Mt. Washburn, Fairy Falls, Lamar Valley, geyser basins
days_list.charge: Supercharger in West Yellowstone overnight; Level 2 at Canyon/Old Faithful while hiking
days_list.sleep: West Yellowstone hotel (or grab an in-park site early for 1 camp night)

days_list.route: Yellowstone → Grand Teton — short drive south
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Colter Bay RV Park or Snake River KOA, Jackson (full hookups; 50-amp overnight charge)

days_list.route: Grand Teton (2 days) — Cascade Canyon, Jenny Lake, Delta Lake
days_list.charge: Jackson Supercharger; Level 2 at Signal Mtn / Colter Bay
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Colter Bay RV Park / Snake River KOA, Jackson (full hookups, 50-amp) + 1 Jackson hotel

days_list.route: Reposition south — toward Salt Lake City; resupply, laundry

stages.name: Colorado Rockies
stages.notes: Trail Ridge Road and the San Juan passes are big climbs — leave town at 90–100% and let regen recover charge on the way down. This is your most flexible stretch: add or trim a San Juan day if you're running ahead or behind.

days_list.route: SLC → Rocky Mountain NP — drive to Estes Park gateway
Rocky Mountain NP (2 days) — Bear Lake, Emerald Lake, Sky Pond, Trail Ridge Rd (>12,000 ft)
days_list.charge: Estes Park — full before Trail Ridge climb

days_list.route: South to the San Juans — drive toward Durango/Ouray
San Juans (2 days) — Ouray, Silverton, alpine hikes ("Switzerland of America")
days_list.sleep: Mountain-town hotel + 1 🏕 Ouray Riverside Resort & RV Park (full hookups, 50-amp)

days_list.route: Colorado → Moab — drive to red-rock country
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Moab KOA or Sun Outdoors Moab (full hookups; 50-amp overnight charge)

days_list.route: Arches & Canyonlands — Delicate Arch (sunrise), Devils Garden, Mesa Arch, Island in the Sky
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Moab KOA / Portal RV Resort (full hookups, 50-amp) + 1 Moab hotel

days_list.route: Moab → Capitol Reef — scenic Hwy 24 west
days_list.charge: Top off in Moab; plan the gap

days_list.route: Capitol Reef → Bryce — uncrowded gem, then Bryce
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Ruby's Inn RV Park, Bryce (full hookups, showers, store; 50-amp overnight charge)

days_list.route: Bryce Canyon — Navajo / Queens Garden combo loop
days_list.sleep: 🏕 Ruby's Inn RV Park, Bryce (full hookups, 50-amp)

days_list.route: Zion (2 days) — The Narrows, Angels Landing (lottery!), Observation Pt
days_list.sleep: Springdale hotel (or 1 🏕 Ouray Riverside Resort & RV Park or Sequoia RV Ranch — full hookups, 50-amp)

days_list.route: Zion → Las Vegas → Nevada interior — I-15 to Vegas (last dense hub), then US-95 north
days_list.sleep: Las Vegas (chain on/near the Strip or off I-15 — avoid cheap off-Strip motels) or Beatty hotel

days_list.route: Nevada → Eastern Sierra (US-395) — up through Tonopah, cut to 395 toward Bishop/Mammoth
days_list.sleep: Bishop or Mammoth hotel (or 🏕 Mammoth Mountain RV Park / Highlands RV Park Bishop — full hookups, 50-amp)

days_list.route: Sierra → Sequoia/Kings Canyon — drive in to the big trees
Sequoia & Kings Canyon (2 days) — General Sherman, Moro Rock, Cedar Grove
days_list.sleep: Hotel + 1 🏕 Sequoia RV Ranch, Three Rivers (full hookups, 50-amp)

days_list.route: → Yosemite — north; plan to arrive before 8 am tomorrow
Yosemite (3 days) — Mist Trail, Yosemite Falls, Cathedral Lakes; Half Dome if lottery hits
days_list.charge: Mariposa / Groveland — charge in gateway, day-trip in

stages.name: California Coast & Oregon
stages.notes: Your easiest charging stretch — enjoy it before the long eastward haul. Use a buffer night here if you're behind; the coast and Bend both reward an extra day.

days_list.route: Yosemite → SF → Big Sur — Hwy 1, the coast, redwoods begin
North to Redwood NP — coast redwoods, tide pools, sea stacks
→ Crater Lake NP, OR — deep-blue caldera, rim drive
Bend, OR (Cascades base) — Smith Rock, Three Sisters, lava country
Columbia Gorge & Mt. Hood — Multnomah Falls, gorge waterfalls
Stage into eastern Oregon — begin the long haul east; charge full
days_list.charge: Bend / Ontario — fill before the gap (charge to 100% — jump-off for the remote crossing)

days_list.route: Bend → Pendleton, OR (~3.5 hr; US-26 through the John Day high desert) — this IS the remote stretch — sparse charging; leave Bend at 100%, ABRP carefully, treat usable range conservatively
days_list.charge: Pendleton/Hermiston Supercharger (I-84 corridor) at the end — this is the move that de-fangs the gap

days_list.route: Pendleton → Coeur d'Alene, ID (~3.5–4 hr; I-84 → Spokane → I-90)
days_list.charge: Well-charged on the interstate spine from here

days_list.route: → Missoula, MT — comfortable interstate driving
Missoula → Kalispell — ~114 mi, one easy hop
Glacier NP (2 days) — Going-to-the-Sun Rd, Highline, Grinnell Glacier, Avalanche Lake
days_list.charge: Charge FULL in Kalispell; day-trip in — no fast charging in park
days_list.sleep: Kalispell hotel; or 🏕 West Glacier KOA (west) / St. Mary KOA (east — 50-amp solves the east-side charging gap)

days_list.route: Buffer / Two Medicine / Many Glacier — hold loose for weather or a place you love
days_list.sleep: 🏕 West Glacier KOA or Johnson's of St. Mary RV (full hookups, 50-amp)

days_list.route: Glacier → east across MT — optional: Theodore Roosevelt NP, ND near Bismarck (uncrowded, but pushes the day long — better as a next-day stop on the run toward Bismarck)
days_list.sleep: Billings hotel (Bozeman if you prefer the mountains)

days_list.route: ND → MN → … → Boston — ~6 legs, each ≤7 hr: Billings → Bismarck (~5.5 hr) → Twin Cities, MN (~6.5 hr) → Chicago area (~6.5 hr) → Cleveland, OH (~5.5 hr) → Syracuse, NY (~5 hr) → Boston (~5 hr). The Syracuse (or Buffalo) overnight is required — Cleveland→Boston direct is ~10 hr. Keep it loose (no-prebook interstate stretch).
